reasoning: The baseline value on March 30, 2025 (latest prior business day) is 6.00. The Baa corporate bond yield recently hit a local peak of 6.06 before correcting to 6.00. 6.00 is towards the upper end of its trailing 12-month range (5.33-6.13) and above the average of recent weeks. Given mean-reversion tendencies and an ongoing Fed easing cycle aimed at capping runaway yields, short-term momentum favors a slight decrease. Statistical trend analyses similarly output a <50% chance of being above 6.00 for the immediate dates. Accordingly, the closest date has a 45% probability of a strict increase. Following the rule that uncertainty increases with horizon length, the probability gradually converges upwards to 0.50 for the 1-year mark.
